text.content: AInchors is moving toward delivering AI consulting services. Platform must eventually support multiple clients without cross-contamination. Explore containerised OpenClaw as the path to SaaS delivery. Key questions to answer: (1) Container model — Docker/Podman per-client container? Shared gateway, isolated agent workspaces? What does OpenClaw's container support actually provide today? (2) OC2 architecture — how do OC2-A and OC2-B (48GB M4 Pro, July 2026) fit into a containerised deployment? Can OC2 run per-client containers with local model inference? (3) Client isolation — data isolation (client data never crosses agent boundaries), access isolation (client can only see their own data), agent isolation (client agents run in separate containers/namespaces). (4) Interim architecture — what can be done on OC1 now before OC2 arrives? Namespace-based isolation? Separate agent workspaces per client? (5) Target architecture — what does a production SaaS deployment look like at 10 clients? 50 clients? (6) Security implications — S4 (least privilege), data sovereignty, AU compliance per client. Deliverable: architecture proposal with interim (OC1 now) and target (OC2 + containers) states. Ken decision gate before any implementation.
